message:
Here's something that surprises a lot of cat parents: if your cat paws food out of the bowl, eats off the floor, or seems weirdly fussy at a full dish, their whiskers might be the reason. 🐱

Whiskers aren't just adorable — they're incredibly sensitive touch sensors that help your cat judge tight spaces, navigate in the dark, and read the world around them. But that sensitivity has a downside. When a cat eats from a narrow, deep bowl, the sides press and drag against those delicate whiskers over and over. For some cats that constant input is genuinely unpleasant — a phenomenon people call "whisker fatigue" — and it can quietly turn mealtime stressful.

The easy fix? Try a wide, shallow bowl or even a flat plate, so their whiskers never brush the sides. Lots of fussy eaters suddenly relax and dig right in. 🍽️💛 (One honest note: not every vet is fully sold on "whisker fatigue" as a diagnosis — but plenty of owners swear their cat eats happier with a wider dish, and it's a harmless, easy thing to try.)

Have you ever switched your cat to a wider bowl? Did it make a difference? 🐾
